lines: Vask glances up. The pen does not stop moving. "What do you need?"

choices.label: The road's clear — both raiders down. Reporting in.
(You already have a contract pending — get out there.)

lines: Vask sets the pen down. "Proving contract. You want a place on the roster, you earn it — same as everyone who's walked through that gate."
"Two bandits raided the Maren farmstead and holed up on the road to the northwest. Go and put both of them down — that's the whole job."
"You won't go alone. Voss has run that road before; he'll go with you. Come back when it's done and I'll sign you on."

choices.label: Understood. Two bandits, northwest. We'll handle it.

lines: Vask hears you out, checks something in her ledger, and nods once — not warmly, but it counts. "Both of them. Good. You did the work and you came back to report it. That's the whole of it."
"You're on the roster as of now — rank Hired Blade. Contracts go up on the board; pay's per head and per job. The Bureau remembers who delivers and who runs, so mind your Standing."

Flat, like a weather report: "And watch yourself out there. The raids have been too coordinated lately — different families, same roads, same nights. That's not the usual rabble. I don't have a theory yet."
She squares the ledger and looks back up at you. "So. You after a contract, or do you need a moment first?"

choices.label: Give me a moment. I'll be back.

lines: Vask sets the pen down. Pulls a slip off the contract pile, runs a thumb along the seal, and slides it across the desk.
"Job's on the slip — bandits, beasts, a recovery, a body to find, whatever's come in. Details up top once you take it. Hired-Blade rate, per the contract terms."
She marks the slip and slides it to the corner of the desk. "Don't be slow about it."
She nods at the door. "TO MISSION up top when you're ready."
She slides the slip back into the pile without comment. Picks the pen up. "Come back when you're ready to work."
She glances at the pile. "You've got a slip with your name on it. Run THAT one. Don't bring me work-in-progress and ask for more."
She closes the ledger. Folds her hands on it. "Show me."
She nods once at whatever proof you give her — she's seen enough to know — and counts coin onto the desk. "Per the contract. Ledger entry's yours."
Vask gives you the half-nod that passes for a goodbye in this office and bends back over the ledger.
